questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "I looked up the word. ___"
questions.answer: Separable — noun between verb and particle is optional.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "I looked it up. ___"
questions.answer: Separable — pronoun must go between verb and particle.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "You can rely on her. ___"
questions.answer: Non-separable — object follows the full phrasal verb.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "I ran into Alice. ___"
questions.answer: Non-separable — you cannot put anything in the middle.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "She came up with a great idea. ___"
questions.answer: Three-part phrasal verb — always non-separable.

questions.prompt: I didn't know the word so I _____ (look up / it) in the dictionary.
questions.answer: looked it up — Separable + pronoun: the pronoun 'it' must go between the verb and the particle.

questions.prompt: She was making _____ (the story / up) — none of it was true.
questions.answer: the story up — Separable + noun: the noun can go before or after the particle. Both positions are correct.

questions.prompt: You can always _____ (rely on / her) to give a good presentation.
questions.answer: rely on her — Non-separable: the object always follows the full phrasal verb 'rely on'.

questions.prompt: He _____ (call back / me) as soon as he got the message.
questions.answer: called me back — Separable + pronoun: 'me' must go between the verb and the particle.

questions.prompt: I _____ (run into / her) at the supermarket yesterday.
questions.answer: ran into her — Non-separable: 'run into' cannot be separated — the object follows the full phrasal verb.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "I looked up it in the dictionary instead of guessing."
questions.answer: With a pronoun, it must go between verb and particle: 'I looked it up in the dictionary.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She called back him as soon as she got the message."
questions.answer: With a pronoun, it must go between verb and particle: 'She called him back as soon as she got the message.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "You can rely her on to give a great presentation."
questions.answer: 'Rely on' is non-separable — the object always follows the full verb: 'You can rely on her to give a great presentation.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "Ran she into her old teacher outside the classroom."
questions.answer: The subject must come before the verb: 'She ran into her old teacher outside the classroom.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "I turned off the lights and then I put on it."
questions.answer: With a pronoun, it must go between verb and particle: 'I put it on', not 'I put on it'.
I looked it up online.
She was making the story up.
You can rely on him.
He called me back later.
She came up with a great idea.
